Video.summary: Do you worry your teen daughter could throw away everything she's worked for - all for young love? Dina says she gave her daughter, Sammie, everything she'd ever wanted, but the 19-year-old is now only interested in one thing: her 20-year-old boyfriend, Chris, who has a criminal record. Despite cutting the teen off financially, Dina says her ultimatums fell flat and fears they may only have pushed Sammie further into the arms of a boyfriend she thinks is bad news and may be dangerous. Sammie says her controlling mother and sheltered upbringing are the real problems - but, can she explain the bruises on her body? Then, Samantha is a teen who was blinded by her love affair with her allegedly abusive boyfriend, Aaron. Don't miss her update and somber words for Sammie, whom she fears may be walking blindly down the perilous path she says she knows too well.
Video.title: Is My Teen Blinded By Love?

Image.alt: Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?
Is My Teen Blinded By Love?

Video.summary: Who will Brad choose: his wife or girlfriend?
Video.title: Torn Between 2 Lovers Part 1

Video.summary: Craig and Cherie say the last half of their 16-year marriage has been anything but happily-ever-after. Cherie says Craig is chauvinistic, verbally abusive and demands sex, which she admits she only gives him about three to four times a year. Craig says Cherie withholds sex to control him, and as a result, he has become a slave to his anger. Cherie fears the tension in their household is taking a toll on their 11-year-old daughter, who is often caught in the crossfire of their fighting. Dr. Phil challenges the couple to a communication test. Can they heal their wounds and rekindle their romance? Then, meet the couple who inspired the movie, The Vow. Shortly after Kim and Krickitt Carpenter were married, a terrible car crash changed everything. Krickitt lay in a coma for weeks, and awoke with no recognition of her husband. The couple shares their inspiring, though oftentimes heartbreaking, journey of falling in love all over again. How did they find their way back to each other? And what is it like seeing their lives played out on the big screen? If you’ve ever wondered whether love can survive the toughest of challenges, you won’t want to miss this show! This show contains strong sexual content. Viewer discretion advised.
Video.title: Complicated Love

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his conversation with Christina, who emailed him desperate for help for her daughter, Tara, who she says can be an angry monster. Christina left the home when Tara was 13 years old, but now, she says she's worried that her daughter is in a toxic situation with the ex-husband she calls narcissistic and his 22-year-old new wife who is 20 weeks pregnant. Today, meet the new wife and find out if Dr. Phil can help this chaotic triangle.
Video.title: Family Triangle Drama (Part 2)

Video.summary: Dr. Phil’s guests say they’re not getting any younger, and the race is on to say “I do.” Sharon is 48 and says her biological clock is ticking away. She recently got engaged to a man whom she acknowledges is flawed, and she says her family doesn’t approve of her husband-to-be. Can she get them to accept her beau before they walk down the aisle, or do her relatives have a reason to be concerned? Then, Shea from the reality show Southern Belles: Louisville says that as she was planning her big day, she learned that her fiancé allegedly cheated. Now that the wedding is off, can Shea pick up the pieces and move on? Plus, a dating coach explains the biggest dating mistakes women make.
Video.title: Race to the Altar

Video.summary: He’s a tough-guy afraid of loosing his freedom. She’s been through two ugly divorces. So when the “M” word comes up, they head for the hills. Join these commitment-challenged singles as Dr. Phil uncovers the reasons why they won’t take the plunge. You’ll also meet a man who, after a four years in a loving relationship, doesn’t see the need to get married. To him it’s just a piece of paper. To her it’s a lot more. Find out if it’s really a “deal breaker.”
Video.title: Commitment Phobia

Video.summary: Would you rather be right or happy? Dr. Phil talks with some right-fighters who can't settle their debate.
Video.title: I Am Right!

Video.summary: You hear about scams all the time - e-mails and voicemails claiming you have won valuable prizes. Dr. Phil is outraged by what some scammers are doing to him, his family and his viewers. One website offers a Dr. Phil quiz to win $5,000 every hour, another says it has the official Dr. Phil personality test, but even more shocking are 800 numbers that take callers' money for the promise of over the phone therapy with Dr. Phil and Robin. But these 800 numbers connect innocent victims with con artists posing as Dr. Phil staff members. The fake staffers ask anywhere from $375 to $1,000 for consultation time that obviously never takes place.
Video.title: DP Scam - Part One

Video.summary: Do you have a nosy loved one in your life snooping through your things, questioning your every move and driving you crazy? Dr. Phil has advice.
Video.title: Mind Your Business

Video.summary: Are deadbeat dads actually dead broke dads? As the American economy struggles, men comprise most of the recent layoffs. In child support cases, are men and women treated equally in the courts, or are the laws out of touch with today's crisis? Dr. Phil's guests, along with Mel Feit, director of the National Center for Men, and Lis Wiehl, author and legal analyst, debate the issue that has many men feeling like the laws are against them.
Video.title: Fathers And Finance

Video.summary: For the first time, a woman meets her soon-to-be daughter-in-law, whom she has called manipulative, immoral and disrespectful.
Video.title: My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance

Image.alt: My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance
My Son Picked the Wrong Fiance

Video.summary: A woman who vanished reveals a dark secret.
Video.title: A Daughter’s Dark Confession

Video.summary: A woman says her ex-husband enables their daughter's addiction.
Video.title: My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child

Image.alt: My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child

Video.summary: Three wives send their husbands to Dr. Phil's Man Camp for an attitude adjustment.
Video.title: Man Camp Part 1

Video.summary: TV Star Nicholas Brendon has been starring in his own private alcohol-fueled drama which has led to multiple arrests and been documented in celebrity magazines, social media and blogs. Nicholas sits down with Dr. Phil -- and abruptly walks offstage. Is Nicholas ready to take the steps needed to save his life and career?
Video.title: TV Star's Alcoholism Spirals

Video.summary: Do you ever feel nervous before posting on social media? Have you ever censored your own thoughts because you worry that you could be criticized or even canceled for posting your beliefs? Dr. Phil and his guests discuss the self-censorship debate on the internet and social media, including the fundamental misunderstanding about the true extent of “freedom of speech” under the First Amendment. Kimberly, a University of Tennessee doctoral candidate, claims her college violated her freedom of speech after a professional conduct committee determined that she had posted unprofessional content to her social media. What rights do colleges have to spy on personal social media posts? Also Tehran Von Ghasri, a comedian who performs on college campuses, says cancel culture gone too far. How does the fear of being canceled affects the jokes he performs for college students or content he posts online? Alexandra, a social media influencer and business owner, says giving opinions about COVID got her censored and three accounts closed. What did she say to get canceled? Dr. Phil also talks with Attorney Robert Shibley, a Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), who says over half of Americans are not saying how they feel for fear of offending others or being canceled. Jessica Levinson, an attorney and a Clinical Professor of Law at Loyola Law School, provides an explanation on the boundaries of the First Amendment. And, Gabrielle Gambrell, a Columbia University and NYU professor, says that social media sites are obligated to monitor hate and dangerous speech. Plus, find out which emojis you are probably using right now are deemed outdated or inappropriate!
Video.title: You Can’t Post That!

Video.summary: Is there an adult under your roof, eating your food and taking advantage of you financially? If yes, then you live with a moocher! It's time to stop the enabling and start the accountability.
Video.title: Cut 'Em Off!

Video.summary: Sixty-four-year-old Josie says she met the love of her life, Victor, on Facebook. Even though he’s Nigerian, 30 years younger, and she’s never physically met him, she insists it’s true love. She’s purchased a ticket to Nigeria and hopes to marry Victor, but her older sister, Carmen, her best friend, Angela, and her 19-year-old son, Anthony, say Josie is acting like a teenage fool. They believe Victor is conning Josie into marrying him for a green card. Dr. Phil took cameras to Nigeria to find Victor to see if he’s telling Josie the truth. Josie’s family says they want Dr. Phil to convince Josie that this relationship will never work. Is Victor who he says he is, and will Josie leave for Nigeria after hearing what Dr. Phil has to say?
Video.title: “Is My Nigerian Boyfriend Scamming Me?”

Video.summary: They’re self-absorbed, spoiled and entitled brats, otherwise known as Generation Me. MTV correspondent Kim Stolz recently hit the streets of New York to find out how the younger generation feels about this label. Don't miss the candid responses. Then, Bradley, 24, says he wants to be rich and drive a Lamborghini, but he only wants to put in 80 percent of the effort. He still relies on his mom, Christine, to pay his rent and other bills. Will Bradley learn to stand on his own two feet? Plus, Monica says her 18-year-old daughter, Courtney, is an “entitlement princess,” and she gives the teen money just to keep the peace. Will mom and daughter get a wake-up call? And Jean Twenge, author of Generation Me, reveals why many young people are narcissistic.
Video.title: Generation Me

Video.summary: Dr. Phil's guests haven't even been married a year and are already wondering if it’s time to call it quits.
Video.title: The Honeymoon’s Over

Video.summary: Contemporary weddings have turned into five-star productions, with couples pulling out all the stops to shine on their special day. But what happens when the bride or groom-to-be starts making diva-like demands? Mike and Jamie agreed to a small Vegas wedding, but that went out the window when Jamie switched her gown three times, rented a limo to drive her one block and upgraded her engagement ring. Mike says that Jamie also made him uninvite all of his friends and cancelled his bachelor party! Are Jamie’s antics a hint of what’s to come in their marriage? Then, Nastassia complains that her husband-to-be, Richard, is cheap. She says he wants her to buy bridal flowers from the grocery store, have her mom sew her gown and even schedule the wedding before year's end so he can get a tax write-off! Will Richard’s tightwad ways force Nastassia to call off the wedding?
Video.title: Nightmare Brides and Grooms

Video.summary: George and Cindy Anthony speak out for the first time about their daughter, Casey's trial.
Video.title: Cindy and George Anthony:The Interview

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he can’t pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says he’s slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joey’s mistress, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isn’t Enough

Video.summary: Do you have someone in your life who's so annoying and frustrating that you just want him or her to go away? What do you do when you're co-parents with someone you can't get along with? And, what if your daughter secretly dated, and then married, a man you despise?
Video.title: Butt Out!

Video.summary: Their job is to protect you. They won't let you walk down the street alone. They call your house five times a day, screen your calls, monitor your every move. No, it's not the government or the F.B.I., it's mothers! Is there such a thing as a mom having too much love for her child? Dr. Phil puts Mom to the test!
Video.title: Overprotective Moms

Video.summary: A stay-at-home mother says she can't control her rage, and she takes it out on her kids. Dr. Phil intervenes for the sake of her five precious children.
Video.title: Another Angry Mom

Video.summary: Some moms don't want to grow up, and others don't want to grow old. Dr. Phil talks to women walking on the wild side and tries to rein them back in!
Video.title: Moms Gone Wild

Video.summary: After a Tennessee mother caused an international uproar when she sent her 7-year-old adopted son on a one-way flight back to Russia, the subject of international adoptions is making front-page headlines. Dr. Phil speaks with moms who say they relate to the Tennessee woman, and unless you walk a mile in their shoes, you have no way of understanding. Jane says she had no idea her 2-year-old daughter, Mikhaela, was profoundly deaf and autistic when she adopted her from the Ukraine eight years ago. Jane says the 10-year-old has multiple behavioral disorders and kicks, bites, punches and tries to hurt herself daily, and is getting too strong for her to handle. And, Kristi says all the love in the world couldn’t have helped the baby girl she adopted from China. Kristi says she wasn’t even close to being qualified to parent her now 14-year-old daughter, Tierney, who lives in a behavioral foster home in order to keep Kristi’s other children safe. If you’re thinking of adopting internationally, or if you’re an adoptive parent in crisis, don’t miss today’s show. Learn how to prepare for a cross-cultural adoption, how to get through the adoption process the correct way and what the experts have to say.
Video.title: Adoption: Return to Sender?

Video.summary: Jessica, a mom of five, sent Dr. Phil a home video so disturbing it brought his wife, Robin, and many audience members to tears. Jessica admits that she’s angry all the time and says she struggles to parent her 7-year-old adopted son, Kristoff. She says she screams at the boy, puts hot sauce in his mouth and places him in a cold shower when he acts up. Attorney and child advocate Areva Martin weighs in. Then, several irate women in the audience give Jessica a piece of their mind.
Video.title: Mommy Confessions

Video.summary: In September 2013, Kelli Stapleton and her 14-year-old daughter, Issy -- who suffers from severe autism -- were found in the family van, suffering from carbon monoxide poisoning. Could Kelli have done the unthinkable? In a Dr. Phil exclusive, the jailed Michigan mom opens up about what happened.
Video.title: Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)

Image.alt: Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his exclusive jailhouse interview with Kelli Stapleton, a mother accused of trying to kill herself and her severely autistic 14-year-old daughter, Issy. Were there any warning signs that she was reaching her breaking point? And, find out how you can help Issy secure a healthy and productive future.
Video.title: Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)

Image.alt: Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)

Video.summary: Sue says her husband of 12 years, Andy, is verbally abusive and that she’s so scared of him, she remains submissive -- including getting drunk and having sex with him -- to keep the peace. She says, “If he’s going to kill me, he’s going to kill me. I feel like I’m already dead.” So, why is she worried that Andy will leave her? Andy admits that he can be a bully, loses his temper and calls Sue vile names, but he says the duties of a wife are to have sex, cook and clean -- and Sue doesn't carry her weight around the house. After breaking up numerous times, why do they keep reuniting? And, hear from Sue’s two adult children. What do they say contributes to the chaos in their mom and Andy’s relationship? Is there hope for this couple’s marriage, or should they cut ties?
Video.title: Scared Of My Spouse
